settings.title: How to Care for Fresh Ink
settings.excerpt: The first two weeks decide how your tattoo looks for the next twenty years.
settings.body:
A great tattoo can still age badly if the healing goes wrong, and almost all of that is in your hands once you leave the chair.

Keep it clean and let it breathe. Wash gently with unscented soap, pat dry, and apply only a thin layer of the balm we give you.

Stay out of pools, baths, and direct sun while it heals, and do not pick at the flaking, however tempting it is. Follow those rules for two weeks and the work we did will settle exactly as it should.

What the two weeks look like

The first few days are the most tender, and then the tattoo starts to flake and itch as it heals, which is normal. Knowing what to expect makes it much easier not to fuss with it.

  • Days one to three, wash gently and keep the balm thin.
  • The middle stretch, expect flaking and resist the urge to pick.
  • The second week, it settles and the itch fades.

Through all of it, the rule is the same. Less is more. Overdoing the balm or covering it too long causes as many problems as neglect does.

When to call us

A little redness and soreness early on is expected. But if you see spreading heat, swelling, or anything that worries you after the first few days, get in touch rather than guessing.

We are always happy to take a look, and we would much rather hear from you early than have a healing problem quietly settle into the work. Care for it well for two weeks and you will be glad of it for decades.
